data.description:
## Why

Audit Reliability finding #4 (MEDIUM). In src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:137-148, when git add or an unhealable git commit fails, cleanup only unlinks the worktree file — the blob stays **staged**, so the *next* successful write (which commits the whole index) durably commits the failed write's phantom event. The claim/transition path already fixed this exact bug (_unstage in _commands/txn.py:439, "drop index (not just disk)"); the general append path never got the fix. Related (audit 2.2): git commit runs before git add's return code is checked, so a failed add can still commit unrelated staged residue under this write's message.

## What

Mirror txn.py in src/rebar/_store/event_append.py: on failure, git reset -q -- <relpath> in addition to unlinking; and check the add return code before invoking commit. Add a regression test that injects a commit failure, performs a subsequent successful write, and asserts the failed event is neither committed nor replayed. Consider a cheap fsck check for staged-index residue in the tracker (audit fsck-gap note).

## Scope

In: src/rebar/_store/event_append.py failure path, tests. Out: txn.py (already correct), broader fsck redesign.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On stage/commit failure the event is removed from the index (git reset) as well as disk
- [ ] git add's return code is checked before git commit runs
- [ ] Regression test: injected commit failure followed by a successful write — the store contains only the successful event; replay and fsck are clean
- [ ] make test green
data.title: event_append: unstage the staged event on stage_and_commit failure (and check git add before commit)
